name: Panel Method
summary: A low-cost aerodynamic analysis method that approximates inviscid flow by distributing singularities over a surface or aerofoil contour.
whenToUseIt: Use it for rapid lift and pressure-distribution estimates in conceptual and preliminary design when full CFD would be too slow or expensive.
steps: Define the geometry as a set of panels.
Apply source and vortex strengths to satisfy boundary conditions.
Solve the influence matrix for the circulation or source distribution.
Recover surface pressure and integrated aerodynamic forces.
Apply viscous corrections or compare against wind-tunnel data where needed.
example: A student evaluating wing twist options for a UAV can run a panel-method tool like XFLR5 to compare lift distribution changes in minutes rather than setting up a full Navier-Stokes solution.
